name: NOAA Coral Reef Watch
publisher: U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
notes: Satellite-derived SST, SST anomaly, Degree Heating Weeks, and bleaching alert levels at ~5 km resolution. Use for current thermal stress, not for observed coral cover.

name: Allen Coral Atlas
publisher: Arizona State University Center for Global Discovery and Conservation Science
notes: Global high-resolution reef habitat maps and near-real-time bleaching detection. Habitat extents are observed; bleaching alerts are model-derived.

name: Global Coral Reef Monitoring Network
notes: In-situ benthic survey aggregations. Use for observed coral cover and historical bleaching mortality, with survey method and year.

name: AIMS Long-Term Monitoring Program
notes: Great Barrier Reef coral cover, bleaching, and crown-of-thorns surveys. Authoritative for GBR site-level observed condition.

name: Reef Life Survey
notes: Diver-conducted fish and invertebrate transects with effort metadata. Provides effort denominators for sighting probabilities at survey sites.

name: Global Biodiversity Information Facility
license: Per-dataset; mostly CC0 or CC BY
notes: Aggregated species occurrence records. Confirms presence and seasonality clustering, never per-dive probability without effort data. Live fallback source for the weekly sightings ingest (scripts/fetch-sightings-live.mjs) when iNaturalist has no research-grade record for a site/species.

name: Ocean Biodiversity Information System
notes: Marine-focused occurrence records. Use alongside GBIF for marine species presence and seasonality.

name: Divers Alert Network
notes: Diver safety guidance, incident data, and medical references. Use for safety and limitations language in beginner/returning flows.

name: PADI
notes: Certification level definitions and training-standard references. Use for skill-level recommendations, not for ecological claims.

name: IUCN Red List of Threatened Species
publisher: International Union for Conservation of Nature
license: Free for non-commercial use under IUCN terms
notes: Authoritative conservation status (LC/NT/VU/EN/CR/EX) and population trend assessments. Use for species rarity and conservation framing.

name: iNaturalist
publisher: California Academy of Sciences & National Geographic Society
license: CC0 / CC BY / CC BY-NC (per record)
notes: Photo- and date-verified research-grade observations from citizen scientists. Primary live source for the weekly sightings ingest (scripts/fetch-sightings-live.mjs): supplies lastConfirmedAt, recentRecordCount, and observed-month seasonality per site/species. Strong recency signal; combine with GBIF/OBIS for presence.

name: Reef Check
notes: Global volunteer reef-monitoring program with standardised benthic and fish protocols. Use for observed reef condition where local surveys exist.

name: Manta Trust IDtheManta Database
notes: Photo-ID database of individual reef and oceanic mantas worldwide. Confirms aggregation site fidelity and seasonality.

name: Wildbook (Sharkbook, Whale Shark, Manta Matcher)
notes: Open photo-ID platforms for individual sharks, whale sharks, and mantas. Documents return visits to specific dive sites.

name: NOAA National Coral Reef Monitoring Program
notes: Standardised biological, climate, and socioeconomic monitoring across US Caribbean and Pacific reefs. Use for observed reef condition.

name: Great Barrier Reef Marine Park Authority
notes: Authoritative manager for the Great Barrier Reef — zoning, reef-health outlook reports, and management plans. Use for GBR site-level travel and conservation guidance.

name: Atlantic and Gulf Rapid Reef Assessment
publisher: AGRRA Program / Perry Institute for Marine Science
notes: Standardised reef survey protocols and dataset for the wider Caribbean. Use for Caribbean coral cover and bleaching observations.

name: International Coral Reef Initiative
notes: Intergovernmental partnership coordinating reef monitoring and protection. Use as a discovery layer for regional reef-status reports.

name: SSI
notes: Alternative training-agency certification framework. Use alongside PADI for skill-level mapping, not for ecological claims.

name: scubaSeason editorial
notes: scubaSeason editorial estimates compiled from operator websites, dive-travel agency listings, airfare aggregators, and direct destination research. Not live prices.

name: DEMA
notes: Industry trade body — used only for travel-industry context, never for ecological or safety claims.

name: NASA PO.DAAC
notes: Physical Oceanography DAAC — canonical archive for SST, sea-surface salinity, sea level, ocean circulation, ocean winds. Hosts MODIS, GHRSST, SMAP, JASON/Sentinel-6, SWOT datasets.

name: Copernicus Marine Service
publisher: Mercator Ocean International for the European Union
license: Copernicus data policy — free and open with attribution (CC BY equivalent)
notes: Near-real-time and forecast SST, currents, chlorophyll, oxygen, pH for global and regional seas. Best single source for live anomaly cards on location pages.

name: NOAA CoastWatch / OceanWatch
notes: ERDDAP-served satellite oceanography — SST, sea-level anomaly, chlorophyll-a, ocean heat content, sea-surface salinity, ocean winds. Programmatic per-coordinate access.

name: NASA Ocean Color (OB.DAAC)
publisher: NASA Goddard Space Flight Center / Ocean Biology Processing Group
notes: Chlorophyll-a, water clarity, primary productivity from MODIS, VIIRS, OLCI, PACE. Use for site-level water-clarity context and productivity claims.

name: Argo float network
notes: Global array of 4,000+ profiling floats measuring temperature, salinity, and biogeochemistry to 2 km depth. Use for ocean-interior temperature trend claims.

name: NOAA NDBC buoy network
notes: Real-time and historical in-situ wave, wind, water-temp, salinity, and atmospheric data from anchored buoys. Use for site-adjacent conditions where a buoy exists.

name: OBIS-SEAMAP
publisher: Duke University Marine Geospatial Ecology Lab / OBIS
notes: Marine megafauna occurrence database — cetaceans, sea turtles, seabirds, sharks. Use alongside GBIF/OBIS for high-quality megafauna sighting records.

name: Happywhale
publisher: Happywhale (Allied Whale / College of the Atlantic partnership)
license: Free for research/non-commercial; commercial use requires permission
notes: Photo-ID database for humpback, orca, sperm, blue, fin, and gray whales. Tracks individual animals across years — supports return-rate and seasonality claims.

name: REEF Volunteer Fish Survey
license: Public data with attribution; commercial use requires permission
notes: 275,000+ diver-conducted fish surveys with standardized methodology across the Americas, Caribbean, and Pacific. Use for diver-confirmed species presence claims with effort data.

name: CoralWatch
notes: Volunteer coral colour-chart survey program with 15+ years of bleaching observations from 100+ countries. Supports observed-bleaching claims at lower-monitoring sites.

name: Ocean Tracking Network
license: Open data with attribution; per-dataset restrictions may apply
notes: Global acoustic telemetry network tracking fish, sharks, turtles, and marine mammals. Use for migratory-corridor and residence-time claims for tagged species.

name: IMOS / AODN
publisher: Integrated Marine Observing System / Australian Ocean Data Network
notes: Australian oceanographic and biological observation data — moorings, gliders, animal tagging, satellite. Authoritative for Australian and Indo-Pacific waters.

name: WoRMS — World Register of Marine Species
notes: Authoritative taxonomic backbone for marine species names. Use when normalizing common-name → scientific-name and for conservation-status lookups.

name: FishBase
notes: Comprehensive fish species reference — biology, distribution, ecology, conservation. Use for species-specific depth ranges, diet, behaviour claims.

name: Atlas of Living Australia
notes: Australian biodiversity aggregator with 130M+ occurrence records. Use for Great Barrier Reef and Coral Sea species claims.

name: Protected Planet (WDPA)
license: Custom — citation required; shapefile redistribution restricted; derived statistics with attribution fine
notes: Canonical global database of marine and terrestrial protected areas. Use to state whether a dive site sits inside an MPA / no-take zone.

name: Marine Protection Atlas (MPAtlas)
notes: Protection-quality layer over WDPA — distinguishes paper-park MPAs from genuinely enforced no-take zones. Use to qualify MPA claims.

name: Global Fishing Watch
notes: AIS-derived fishing-effort data globally at 0.1° resolution. Use to indicate fishing pressure near dive sites and to support 'sits inside / outside fishing zone' claims.

name: Global Mangrove Watch
publisher: JAXA + Aberystwyth University + UNEP-WCMC + Wetlands International
notes: Annual global mangrove extent and change layers since 1996 from satellite + ground truth. Use for mangrove-adjacent reef claims and habitat-decline context.

name: Green Fins
notes: UN-endorsed directory and standards for sustainable dive operators. Use to flag verified-responsible operators in trip recommendations.

name: WRI Reefs at Risk Revisited
notes: Comprehensive global reef-threat assessment combining local and global pressures. Use for regional risk framing alongside in-situ surveys.

name: Ocean Health Index
notes: Annual composite index of ocean health by EEZ covering 10 goals (food provision, carbon storage, biodiversity, etc.). Use for country-level ocean-health context.

name: NOAA Tides & Currents (CO-OPS)
notes: Authoritative US tide predictions, water levels, currents, and meteorological observations. REST API, no auth. Use for tide-window claims and current-strength context at US-territorial sites.

name: IBTrACS
publisher: NOAA NCEI + WMO + global tropical-cyclone agencies
notes: International Best Track Archive — every named tropical cyclone since 1842. Use for historical hurricane / typhoon / cyclone impact context at affected dive locations.

name: ECMWF Open Data
publisher: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts
notes: Global numerical-weather-prediction outputs at 0.25° resolution. Use for forward-looking wind, wave, and water-temp conditions cards.

name: ERA5 reanalysis (Copernicus CDS)
license: Copernicus data policy — free and open with attribution
notes: Hourly atmospheric and oceanographic reanalysis from 1940 to present. Use for climatological 'typical conditions in October at this location' claims.

name: HYCOM ocean model
license: U.S. Government Work (public release, Distribution A)
notes: Global hybrid-coordinate ocean circulation model. Use for currents, temperature/salinity profiles, and ocean-state forecasts at sub-coastal resolution.

name: NCEI Marine Microplastics
publisher: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information
notes: Global database of microplastic concentrations in seawater, sediment, and biota. Use for 'how plasticky is this water' framing at coastal locations.

name: NOAA ERMA + IncidentNews
publisher: NOAA Office of Response and Restoration
notes: Environmental Response Management Application and incident timeline of US oil spills + chemical releases. Use for active-incident context near US dive sites.

name: EMODnet Chemistry
publisher: European Marine Observation and Data Network
notes: European seawater chemistry — nutrients, contaminants, microplastics, oxygen. Use for water-quality framing at Mediterranean / North Atlantic / Baltic sites.

name: NOAA HAB Operational Forecast
notes: Harmful algal bloom forecasts and bulletins for US waters. Use for active-bloom warnings near US dive locations.

name: HAEDAT — Harmful Algae Event Database
publisher: IOC-UNESCO Intergovernmental Panel on Harmful Algal Blooms
notes: Global registry of HAB events with toxin, species, and impact records since 1985. Use for historical-HAB context at any coastal site.

name: GOA-ON — Global Ocean Acidification Observing Network
notes: Global pH and carbonate-chemistry observations from moorings + cruises. Use for ocean-acidification claims at the reef-health layer.

name: NOAA Mussel Watch
publisher: NOAA National Centers for Coastal Ocean Science
notes: Longest-running US coastal contaminant monitoring — heavy metals, PCBs, PAHs in shellfish tissue back to 1986. Use for historical-contamination context.

name: GEBCO bathymetry grid
publisher: General Bathymetric Chart of the Oceans (IHO + IOC-UNESCO)
notes: Global ocean-floor depth grid at 15-arcsecond resolution. Use for depth-context claims at any dive site, especially walls, dropoffs, and pinnacles.

name: Smithsonian Global Volcanism Program
notes: Authoritative database of Holocene volcanoes worldwide with eruption history. Use for volcanic-origin context at island and seamount sites (Galápagos, Hawaii, Indonesia, Solomons).

name: Naval History and Heritage Command (DANFS)
publisher: US Naval History and Heritage Command
notes: Dictionary of American Naval Fighting Ships — authoritative service histories for US Navy wrecks. Use for wreck dive pages (USS Liberty, SS President Coolidge, Chuuk fleet, Bikini Atoll).

name: USGS Earthquake Catalog
notes: Global earthquake catalog with magnitude, depth, location. REST API. Use for seismic-impact context at sites near active fault zones (Indonesia, Japan, Philippines, Aegean).

name: NOAA ENC Direct
notes: Electronic Navigational Charts including wreck and obstruction overlays. Replaces the retired AWOIS database. Use for wreck-position and obstruction lookups in US waters.

name: NOAA Maritime Heritage Program
publisher: NOAA Office of National Marine Sanctuaries
notes: Underwater cultural heritage protection within US National Marine Sanctuaries — wreck preservation status, archaeology, regulations. Use for wreck-protection framing.

name: OpenSeaMap
notes: Open nautical chart tiles with crowd-sourced harbours, lights, wrecks, and buoys. Use only as a cross-reference layer, not as authoritative wreck-identity data.

name: IPCC Special Report on the Ocean and Cryosphere (SROCC)
notes: The peer-reviewed reference for climate impacts on the ocean. Use to frame long-horizon outlook claims with appropriate confidence levels.
